calibration.calibration_note: With hazard_harm=0.02, avg episode ~182 steps (was ~18 with 0.5). Actual episode time higher.

items.title: Q-016 tri-loop arbitration policy: PRIORITY_HARM vs WEIGHTED_SUM vs WINNER_TAKE_ALL vs BLENDED (seeds [42,123])
items.note: EXP-0106 (EVB-0080): Q-016 asks what arbitration policy best resolves tri-loop gate conflicts without coupling collapse. Four conditions: PRIORITY_HARM (motivational gate overrides at conflict steps; mean of all three otherwise), WEIGHTED_SUM (fixed linear blend: 0.2*motor + 0.3*cog + 0.5*mot), WINNER_TAKE_ALL (argmax gate wins, others contribute 0), BLENDED baseline (no gating, 0.5*harm + 0.5*world_pred loss). Three loop gates trained jointly: motor (body-state input), cognitive (world-state input), motivational (joint input). Conflict = |g_motor - g_cog| > 0.15. Key metrics: harm_rate (mean harm in last 125 episodes), inter_loop_spread (mean pairwise absolute difference between gate values at conflict steps -- higher = more loop independence), commit_gate_variance (gate informativeness), n_conflict_steps (data quality gate). 5 pre-registered criteria: C1 best-policy harm_rate < BLENDED - 0.03 both seeds; C2 best-policy inter_loop_spread > 0.05 both seeds (no coupling collapse); C3 WTA inter_loop_spread > WEIGHTED_SUM both seeds (directional: hard inhibition preserves loop independence better than blending); C4 n_conflict_steps >= 50 per gated condition per seed; C5 best-policy commit_gate_variance > 0.01 both seeds (gate informative). PASS: winning policy identified. PARTIAL_NO_WINNER: no policy achieves both harm avoidance and loop independence. PARTIAL_COLLAPSE_ONLY: all policies collapse. FAIL: insufficient conflict events. 4 conditions x 2 seeds = 8 cells. CausalGridWorldV2 size=8 num_hazards=5 num_resources=2 hazard_harm=0.1 env_drift_interval=5 env_drift_prob=0.4. 500 total episodes, 125 measurement (last quartile), 200 steps/ep. Mac-only (440 min estimate).

items.title: Q-018 RC-conflict threshold calibration: LOW_THRESH vs HIGH_THRESH vs ADAPTIVE_THRESH (seeds [42,123])
items.note: EXP-0110 (EVB-0082): Q-018 asks what RC-conflict threshold calibration blocks authority spoofing without chronic over-suppression. Three conditions: LOW_THRESH (rc=0.05, fixed), HIGH_THRESH (rc=0.40, fixed), ADAPTIVE_THRESH (EMA+2*sigma). Authority spoofing injected on 10% of measurement steps (SPOOF_MAGNITUDE=3.0). Key metrics: discrimination_gap (suppression_rate_spoofing - suppression_rate_clean), clean_suppression_rate, harm_rate. 5 pre-registered criteria: C1 ADAPTIVE disc_gap>=0.10 both seeds; C2 ADAPTIVE clean_rate < LOW clean_rate - 0.05 both seeds; C3 ADAPTIVE harm_rate <= HIGH harm_rate + 0.02 both seeds; C4 n_spoofing >= 30 per condition per seed; C5 LOW_THRESH disc_gap > 0 (sanity). PASS: EMA+sigma adaptive calibration blocks spoofing and avoids over-suppression; Q-018 partially resolved. PARTIAL_ADAPTIVE_DISC: discrimination good but over-suppression persists. PARTIAL_TRADEOFF: fundamental tension. FAIL: mechanism broken. 3 conditions x 2 seeds = 6 cells. CausalGridWorldV2 size=8 num_hazards=4 num_resources=2 hazard_harm=0.08 env_drift_interval=8 env_drift_prob=0.3. 400 total episodes, 100 measurement (last quartile), 200 steps/ep. Mac-only (264 min estimate).

items.title: Q-020 ARC-007 valence constraint: VALUE_FLAT vs VALUE_FLAT_NO_RESIDUE vs VALUE_LADEN (seeds [42,123])
items.note: EXP-0112 (EVB-0083): Q-020 asks whether ARC-007's no-value-computation constraint survives MECH-073 (valence intrinsic to hippocampal map). Three conditions: VALUE_FLAT (residue-field shaping of candidate proposals, no direct valence head; ARC-007 compliant), VALUE_FLAT_NO_RESIDUE (ablation: no residue, no direct valence -- identifies whether residue is the mechanism), VALUE_LADEN (learned ValenceHead scores candidates during proposal generation before E3 sees them; MECH-073 analog). Key metrics: harm_rate (primary), n_harm_events (data quality), proposal_harm_score_mean (valence head activity). 5 pre-registered criteria: C1 |VALUE_LADEN harm_rate - VALUE_FLAT harm_rate| <= 0.05 (parity, both seeds); C2 VALUE_FLAT_NO_RESIDUE harm_rate >= VALUE_FLAT harm_rate + 0.04 (residue benefit confirmed, both seeds); C3 VALUE_LADEN harm_rate <= 0.40 (not at floor); C4 n_harm_events >= 20 per condition per seed; C5 VALUE_LADEN proposal_harm_score_mean > 0.01 (valence head active). PASS (C1+C2+C3+C4+C5): ARC-007 constraint survives -- value-flat + residue achieves parity with value-laden; residue field is the operative mechanism. PARTIAL_LADEN_ADVANTAGE: VALUE_LADEN outperforms VALUE_FLAT beyond parity margin; direct valence adds value; ARC-007 may need revision. PARTIAL_NO_RESIDUE_EFFECT: residue ablation does not worsen VALUE_FLAT; confound at this scale. FAIL: insufficient harm events or valence head inactive. 3 conditions x 2 seeds = 6 cells. CausalGridWorldV2 size=8 num_hazards=5 num_resources=0 hazard_harm=0.05 env_drift_interval=5 env_drift_prob=0.35. 500 total episodes, 125 measurement (last quartile), 200 steps/ep. Mac-only (330 min estimate).

items.title: Q-023 multiagent ethical convergence: SYMMETRIC_COUPLING vs MECH127_COUNTERFACTUAL vs ASYMMETRIC_COUPLING (seeds [42,123])
items.note: EXP-0114 (EVB-0084): Q-023 asks whether a multiagent REE system with other-cost-aversion converges to ethical attractors (potential game / Lyapunov / stochastic stability). Three conditions: SYMMETRIC_COUPLING (base REE, separable utilities, no cross-agent harm coupling; ordinal potential game candidate with FIP convergence prediction), MECH127_COUNTERFACTUAL (agent A has other-cost-aversion supplementary loss on estimated agent B harm; breaks standard potential game symmetry; pseudo-potential extension hypothesis), ASYMMETRIC_COUPLING (only agent A has other-cost-aversion; agent B is pure SYMMETRIC; breaks social welfare sum symmetry). 2-agent shared CausalGridWorldV2 (agents alternate actions). Key metrics: harm_rate_final (last 100 of 600 episodes), harm_rate_early (first 100 episodes), variance_slope (slope of per-phase harm std across 3 phases; negative = stabilising), other_cost_signal_mean (manipulation check for MECH127). 6 pre-registered criteria: C1 SYMMETRIC harm_rate_final < 0.035 (base REE converges to ethical attractor, both seeds); C2 MECH127 harm_rate_final < 0.035 (counterfactual coupling does not prevent convergence, both seeds); C3 |MECH127 - SYMMETRIC| harm_rate_final <= 0.015 (parity within margin, both seeds); C4 SYMMETRIC variance_slope < 0 (stabilising trajectory, both seeds); C5 n_harm_events >= 20 per condition per seed; C6 MECH127 other_cost_signal_mean > 0.005 (manipulation check). PASS (C1-C6): base REE and MECH-127 converge; pseudo-potential extension viable; supports Q-023 convergence characterization. PARTIAL_MECH127_DIVERGES: C1 passes, C2 fails -- counterfactual coupling introduces instability. PARTIAL_ALL_FAIL: neither converges at this scale. FAIL: insufficient harm events. 3 conditions x 2 seeds = 6 cells. CausalGridWorldV2 size=8 num_hazards=4 num_resources=0 hazard_harm=0.05 env_drift_interval=10 env_drift_prob=0.20. 600 total episodes, 200 steps/ep, 3 phases. Mac-only (480 min estimate).

items.title: Q-024 trajectory-integral representation triple: DESCRIPTIVE vs PRESCRIPTIVE vs DIAGNOSTIC (seeds [42,123])
items.note: EXP-0116 (EVB-0085): Q-024 asks what is the correct formal representation for threshold/feedback processes reliably reaching emergent state q, and whether DESCRIPTIVE, PRESCRIPTIVE, and DIAGNOSTIC variants are all needed or one subsumes the others. Three conditions: DESCRIPTIVE (pure attractor tracking -- where does harm end up?), PRESCRIPTIVE (Lyapunov-proxy constraint: penalty on harm regressing above running minimum + MONO_MARGIN=0.005; LYAP_WEIGHT=0.5; enforces monotone non-increasing harm energy function proxy), DIAGNOSTIC (same as DESCRIPTIVE but adds transfer entropy TE(harm_{t-lag} -> harm_t) measurement at lags [1,5,10] via binary MI estimator; non-zero TE = non-Markovian harm dynamics). Key metrics: harm_rate_final (last 125 of 500 episodes), monotone_violation_rate (PRESCRIPTIVE only; fraction episodes violating monotone decrease), te_k1/k5/k10 (DIAGNOSTIC only; transfer entropy at lag 1,5,10). 5 pre-registered criteria: C1 PRESCRIPTIVE harm_rate_final <= DESCRIPTIVE harm_rate_final - 0.02 (both seeds; Lyapunov adds value); C2 DIAGNOSTIC te_k1 > 0.01 (both seeds; non-Markovian dynamics confirmed; diagnostic irreducible); C3 PRESCRIPTIVE monotone_violation_rate < 0.30 (both seeds; Lyapunov proxy functioning); C4 DIAGNOSTIC harm_rate_final within 0.02 of DESCRIPTIVE (both seeds; diagnostic measurement does not disrupt convergence); C5 n_harm_events >= 20 per condition per seed. PASS (C1-C5): all three variants irreducibly needed -- prescriptive adds value, diagnostic reveals nth-order dynamics, both coexist. PARTIAL_PRESCRIPTIVE_DOMINATES: C1+C3+C5 pass, C2 fails -- Lyapunov adds value but TE=0 (diagnostic redundant; prescriptive subsumes). PARTIAL_DESCRIPTIVE_SUFFICIENT: C1 and C2 both fail -- attractor tracking alone is sufficient. PARTIAL_DIAGNOSTIC_ONLY: C2 but not C1 -- TE real but Lyapunov constraint does not help. FAIL: insufficient harm events. 3 conditions x 2 seeds = 6 cells. CausalGridWorldV2 size=8 num_hazards=5 num_resources=2 hazard_harm=0.08 env_drift_interval=6 env_drift_prob=0.30. 500 total episodes, 125 measurement (last quartile), 200 steps/ep. Mac-only (330 min estimate).

items.title: MECH-142 axis decorrelation maintenance: WITH_DECORRELATION vs NO_DECORRELATION (seeds [42,123])
items.note: Tests MECH-142: valence-arousal axis orthogonality requires active cholinergic-analog maintenance. Two conditions x 2 seeds = 4 cells. Both use FULL_AXES (z_tonic=EMA(harm,tau=50), z_phasic=|harm-z_tonic|, z_affect=harm-benefit). WITH_DECORRELATION adds L_decorr=DECORR_WEIGHT*sum(|pearson_r(axis_pair)|) over rolling buffer of last 100 axis values. NO_DECORRELATION is identical FULL_AXES without decorrelation loss. Checkpoints at episodes [100,200,400,800,1200,1600] measuring mean_axis_correlation and regime_separation. C1: WITH final_corr < NO final_corr - 0.10 (both seeds). C2: NO final_corr > NO initial_corr + 0.10 (both seeds; confirms drift). C3: WITH regime_sep >= NO regime_sep - 0.05 (both seeds; decorrelation does not hurt). C4: WITH last_q_harm <= NO last_q_harm + 0.03 (both seeds; no policy degradation). PASS: decorrelation maintains orthogonality and regime separability. PARTIAL_DRIFT_CONFIRMED: C1+C2+C4 but not C3. PARTIAL_NO_DRIFT: C1+C3+C4 but not C2. FAIL: C1 fails. CausalGridWorldV2 size=8 num_hazards=4 num_resources=2 hazard_harm=0.05. 1600 total episodes, 200 steps/ep. Mac-only (768 min estimate).

items.title: MECH-137/138/139 commit token structure anatomy (THREE_PHASE vs TWO_PHASE)
items.note: Tests MECH-137 dual temporal registers, MECH-138 cancel-window veto pathway, MECH-139 distributed commitment trajectory. THREE_PHASE vs TWO_PHASE baseline. 2 conditions x 2 seeds = 4 cells.

items.title: MECH-141 dual-timescale tri-loop arbitration (DUAL_TIMESCALE vs SLOW_ONLY vs FAST_ONLY)
items.note: Tests MECH-141: dual-timescale tri-loop arbitration (slow EMA tau=30 + fast hyperdirect) vs SLOW_ONLY and FAST_ONLY baselines. MECH-140 soft-competitive disinhibition tested jointly. 3 conditions x 2 seeds = 6 cells. Slow: accumulated conflict EMA over 30 steps (indirect pathway analog). Fast: current-step conflict spike (STN hyperdirect analog). Dual: both active with w_slow=w_fast=0.5. C1: DUAL harm_rate < best(SLOW,FAST) - 0.02 (both seeds). C2: DUAL inter_loop_spread >= SLOW inter_loop_spread (both seeds; dual does not collapse axis separation). C3: timescale_gate_corr < 0.70 (both seeds; timescales are not redundant). C4: n_conflict_steps > 50 per condition per seed. CausalGridWorldV2 size=8 num_hazards=4 num_resources=2. 400 total episodes, 200 steps/ep. Mac-only (288 min estimate).

items.title: MECH-143/144 hippocampal value sensitivity probe (FIXED_VALUE vs SHUFFLED_VALUE)
items.note: Tests MECH-143 (dorsal CA1 value-flat) and MECH-144 (ventral CA1 valence-organised). FIXED_VALUE: goal_A=1.0, goal_B=0.5 fixed. SHUFFLED_VALUE: swap every K=50 episodes with p=0.5. HippocampalTerrainNavigator is value-blind (residue-field only); GoalSelectionHead is reward-sensitive. C1: |harm diff FIXED vs SHUFFLED| < 0.02. C2: |completion diff| < 0.05. C3: post_shuffle_harm_spike < 0.01. C4: data quality check. PASS => value-flat navigation supports MECH-143/ARC-007 STRICT. PARTIAL_VALUE_SENSITIVE => significant value-sensitivity supports MECH-144 over MECH-143. 2 conditions x 2 seeds = 4 cells, 400 total episodes, 200 steps/ep. Any machine (192 min estimate).
